sentence_reviews.text: Task decomposition breaks a broad objective into ordered or partially ordered subgoals whose outputs, dependencies, and stopping conditions can be managed explicitly.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA problem solving, planning, and hierarchical decomposition material.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited source supports decomposition and planning structure; outputs and stopping-condition wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning is to connect Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* to downstream capabilities including PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning, Observe–plan–act agent loops.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA planning/search/agent material and general probabilistic-modeling background; chain-of-thought connection is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: The planning and search role is supported, but the LLM chain-of-thought and observe-plan-act framing is not directly stated.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA classical and hierarchical planning material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Planning formalisms directly use goals, subgoals, and decomposition.
Planning agents support the claim, but the exact observe-plan-act wording is ontology synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*.
sentence_reviews.notes: Search cost is supported; self-evaluation/confident-error and chain-of-thought dependency are broader than the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning uses Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA reasoning/planning background; chain-of-thought-specific evidence is not cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: LLM task decomposition may use chain-of-thought, but hierarchical reasoning in general does not require it.

sentence_reviews.text: Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning uses Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Task planning often uses search over states/subtasks, though BFS/DFS/A* are examples rather than always required.

sentence_reviews.text: Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA symbolic representation/planning background; program/AST-specific evidence is not cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: The packet sources do not support programs/AST/IR as a reusable ingredient for task decomposition in general.

edge_reviews.notes: Plausible for LLM implementations, but not a core dependency of task decomposition generally.
Search is central to many planning/decomposition methods; exact dependency label is broader than direct evidence.
The cited evidence does not support programs/AST/IR as a common dependency.
